title: Segmented Path Loader
prompt:
Create a production-ready Segmented Path Loader using React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. It should communicate a bounded import task through seven black path segments on a white Bento tile; one mint segment advances at a time and a numeric label reads “Preparing 4 of 7.” The system is light and precise: white surface, #F4F5F6 surround, #111111 labels, #4A4A4F support copy, #ECECEE border, 22px radius, soft shadow, and mint as its only bright accent. Do not use gradients.

This is a loader, so a gentle repeated sequence is permitted. Each active segment should brighten and lift 3px over 260ms with ease-out, hold for 420ms, then yield to the next segment; use a spring of stiffness 330 and damping 28 only for the active marker. The progress indication must be exposed with role progressbar, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, and readable aria-valuetext. Provide a static initial SSR state, never claim completion without user data, and avoid noisy live announcements—one polite text update per segment is enough.

Make it legible in a 320px card and in a wide preview, with all segments visible and no fixed elements. Maintain contrast even when inactive segments are muted. Prefer transform and opacity, avoid remote images, storage, random values, or network calls. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the current segment without looping. Include full client TypeScript React, Tailwind and Framer Motion source plus a pure CSS fallback and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* Suggested HTML:
*/ .uiwiki-segmented-loader{display:flex;gap:8px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.uiwiki-segmented-loader i{flex:1;height:32px;border-radius:8px;background:#111;opacity:.25}.uiwiki-segmented-loader .is-active{background:#53e8d4;opacity:1;animation:segment-path .94s ease-in-out infinite}@keyframes segment-path{50%{transform:translateY(-3px)}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.uiwiki-segmented-loader .is-active{animation:none}}

demoCode:
